Title: Give the example pages site chrome and show the full workflow inline
Body:
<!-- STATUS: Drafted on 2026-07-22 -->
<!-- GITHUB_PROPERTIES: Milestone: v1.0.0, Labels: enhancement, Assignee: @me -->

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#1 · Follows #28 (thumbnail gallery) · Blocks the v1.0.0 docs-site gate

## Summary

The nine `Example_*` pages are the package's showcase surface — every gallery thumbnail and README link lands on one — but they render as bare `rmarkdown::html_document` output with no site chrome: no way back to the package home or reference index, a hand-maintained prev/next ring that has to be re-stitched by hand whenever a chart is added, and a `<style>` block copy-pasted nine times. They also describe the report workflow without showing it, so a reader has to leave the page to see what `qt_explorer.yaml` actually contains.

## Proposed Solution

- **Shared chrome.** A single `pkgdown/menus/examples/util/` fragment, injected via `includes: before_body`, carrying the branded header band (package home, reference index, safety.viz, GitHub) and all page CSS. `gsm.utils::build_assets()` globs `*.Rmd` non-recursively, so files under `util/` are never rendered as pages of their own.
- **Generated navigation.** Sibling `Example_*.Rmd` front matter (`title`, `description`, `index`) is the single source of truth: the chip strip and prev/next pager are derived from it at knit time, so adding a chart wires itself in and the ring cannot drift.
- **The full workflow, collapsed.** Each page embeds the verbatim contents of its `inst/workflow/3_reports/*.yaml` in a `<details>` disclosure, read from the installed package so the displayed YAML is always the shipped YAML.
- **Provenance line.** Package version, vendored safety.viz bundle version, and render date, generated rather than typed.

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage of the helper contract (manifest ordering, nav ring, verbatim YAML) and of every page being wired to the shared chrome
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — pages rendered and reviewed in the browser, then published as RC evidence on the hub
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

---

Title: Add Widget_QtExplorer htmlwidget consuming the safety.viz bundle

<!-- STATUS: Drafted on 2026-07-21 21:48 EDT -->

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Unblocked by the safety.viz v1.4.0 release (QT Explorer Phase 1 shipped via safety.viz #68; Phase 2 tracked at jwildfire/obot.roadmap#37)

## Summary

Add `Widget_QtExplorer`, the QT/QTc safety explorer htmlwidget — the ninth of the nine renderers exported by the vendored safety.viz v1.4.0 bundle. Together with `Widget_AeExplorer` this closes the gap between the vendored bundle and the R bindings, so v1.0.0 ships a binding for every renderer safety.viz supports.

## Proposed Solution

- `Widget_QtExplorer()` R function + `inst/htmlwidgets/Widget_QtExplorer.js`/`.yaml` on the established pattern: `BuildWidgetPayload()` validation against a vendored `qt-explorer.json` data contract, module lifecycle driven from `renderValue`/`resize`.
- Vendor `inst/schema/qt-explorer.json` from safety.viz v1.4.0.
- Ship the ADEG example dataset: vendor `inst/extdata/adeg.csv.gz` (the same pharmaverseadam-derived ECG extract the safety.viz demo uses — QTcF / QTcB / Heart Rate for 254 participants across Placebo / Xanomeline Low / Xanomeline High) and extend `ExampleData()` with an `"adeg"` option. The existing `adbds` extract carries no ECG parameters, so qt-explorer cannot be demonstrated without it.
- Report workflow `inst/workflow/3_reports/qt_explorer.yaml`, runner `inst/examples/qt-explorer.R`, pkgdown example page, and qcthat-linked tests, mirroring the other lanes.
- Scope is safety.viz QT Explorer **Phase 1** (central tendency with Δ/ΔΔ and CIs against ICH E14 thresholds, outlier scatter, categorical views). Phase 2 features (QTcI, PR/QRS, drill-down, hysteresis) land in safety.viz first and are out of scope here.

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue in the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — rendered pkgdown example page reviewed on the PR preview site
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

---

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Unblocked by the safety.viz v1.4.0 release (QT Explorer Phase 1 shipped via safety.viz #68; Phase 2 tracked at jwildfire/obot.roadmap#37)

## Summary

Add `Widget_QtExplorer`, the QT/QTc safety explorer htmlwidget — the ninth of the nine renderers exported by the vendored safety.viz v1.4.0 bundle. Together with `Widget_AeExplorer` this closes the gap between the vendored bundle and the R bindings, so v1.0.0 ships a binding for every renderer safety.viz supports.

## Proposed Solution

- `Widget_QtExplorer()` R function + `inst/htmlwidgets/Widget_QtExplorer.js`/`.yaml` on the established pattern: `BuildWidgetPayload()` validation against a vendored `qt-explorer.json` data contract, module lifecycle driven from `renderValue`/`resize`.
- Vendor `inst/schema/qt-explorer.json` from safety.viz v1.4.0.
- Ship the ADEG example dataset: vendor `inst/extdata/adeg.csv.gz` (the same pharmaverseadam-derived ECG extract the safety.viz demo uses — QTcF / QTcB / Heart Rate for 254 participants across Placebo / Xanomeline Low / Xanomeline High) and extend `ExampleData()` with an `"adeg"` option. The existing `adbds` extract carries no ECG parameters, so qt-explorer cannot be demonstrated without it.
- Report workflow `inst/workflow/3_reports/qt_explorer.yaml`, runner `inst/examples/qt-explorer.R`, pkgdown example page, and qcthat-linked tests, mirroring the other lanes.
- Scope is safety.viz QT Explorer **Phase 1** (central tendency with Δ/ΔΔ and CIs against ICH E14 thresholds, outlier scatter, categorical views). Phase 2 features (QTcI, PR/QRS, drill-down, hysteresis) land in safety.viz first and are out of scope here.

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue in the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — rendered pkgdown example page reviewed on the PR preview site
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

Body:
<!-- STATUS: Drafted on 2026-07-21 21:48 EDT -->
<!-- GITHUB_PROPERTIES: Milestone: v1.0.0, Labels: enhancement, Assignee: @me -->

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Unblocked by the safety.viz v1.3.0 release (aeExplorer shipped via safety.viz #60)

## Summary

Add `Widget_AeExplorer`, the adverse-event explorer htmlwidget — the eighth of the nine renderers exported by the vendored safety.viz v1.4.0 bundle. The bundle already ships `SafetyViz.aeExplorer`; gsm.safety has no binding for it, so the v1.0.0 release would expose only seven of nine available renderers.

## Proposed Solution

- `Widget_AeExplorer()` R function + `inst/htmlwidgets/Widget_AeExplorer.js`/`.yaml` on the established pattern: `BuildWidgetPayload()` validation against a vendored `ae-explorer.json` data contract, module lifecycle driven from `renderValue`/`resize`.
- Vendor `inst/schema/ae-explorer.json` from safety.viz v1.4.0.
- Report workflow `inst/workflow/3_reports/ae_explorer.yaml`, runner `inst/examples/ae-explorer.R`, pkgdown example page, and qcthat-linked tests, mirroring the other lanes.
- Re-vendor `inst/extdata/adae.csv.gz` from the safety.viz reference demo dataset so it carries the 37 placeholder rows for AE-free participants (254 participants total, 217 with events). The ae-explorer data contract (AE-DATA-001) requires one placeholder row per AE-free participant so the population denominator is right; the current 1,122-row extract drops them, which would inflate every prevalence rate. Placeholder rows are identified by a blank System Organ Class and never render as events, so `Widget_AeTimelines` is unaffected.

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue in the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — rendered pkgdown example page reviewed on the PR preview site
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Design: inline Design section on the parent requirement · Depends on the six Widget_* sub-issues

## Summary

Modernize the report workflows: rewrite the `inst/workflow/3_reports/` scaffolds against the new `Widget_*()` renderers so each safety.viz chart has a runnable gsm-idiom workflow, and retire the safetyCharts-era scaffolds.

## Proposed Solution

- One report workflow YAML per shipped renderer (histogram, shift plot, delta-delta, results over time, outlier explorer, AE timelines) following the gsm `meta`/`spec`/`steps` idiom, ending in a `Widget_X()` + `htmlwidgets::saveWidget()` render step.
- Runnable via `gsm.core::RunWorkflow()` against the vendored demo extracts; a runnable script per workflow lands in `inst/examples/`.
- The `ae_explorer`/`hep_explorer` safetyCharts scaffolds are removed (hepExplorer returns as a widget when safety.viz ships it — tracked as safety.viz PR #44).

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue per the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — rendered example page published as RC evidence on the hub

Body:
<!-- STATUS: Drafted on 2026-07-12 18:20 EDT -->

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Design: inline Design section on the parent requirement · Depends on the vendored safety.viz v1.1.0 bundle (bundle/infrastructure sub-issue)

## Summary

Add `Widget_AeTimelines`, an interactive per-participant adverse event timeline, rendered by the safety.viz `aeTimelines` module. Mirrors how gsm.kri wraps gsm.viz charts.

## Proposed Solution

- `inst/htmlwidgets/Widget_AeTimelines.js` + `Widget_AeTimelines.yaml` on the vendored `inst/htmlwidgets/lib/safety.viz-1.1.0/` bundle (the gsm.kri precedent — no node toolchain at install time).
- `Widget_AeTimelines()` R function maps a long data.frame plus a settings list onto the module's JSON data contract (`src/data/schema/ae-timelines.json` in safety.viz), then drives the module lifecycle API (`init`/`setData`/`setSettings`/`render`/`resize`/`destroy`) from the widget's `renderValue`/`resize` hooks.
- Roxygen `@examples` and the matching report workflow use the canonical pharmaverseadam demo extract (`adae`) vendored with the package.

## Example Usage

```r
Widget_AeTimelines(
  dfResults = ExampleData("adae"),
  lSettings = list(
    # settings keys per the ae-timelines data contract
  )
)
```

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue per the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)

<!-- GITHUB_PROPERTIES: Milestone: v1.0.0, Labels: enhancement, Assignee: @me -->

Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#28 · Design: inline Design section on the parent requirement

## Summary

Vendor the safety.viz v1.1.0 `dist/` bundle into gsm.safety and stand up the shared htmlwidget infrastructure all six `Widget_*()` bindings build on; retire the legacy safetyCharts/Tendril bridge in the same move.

## Proposed Solution

- `inst/htmlwidgets/lib/safety.viz-1.1.0/safety.viz.js` vendored byte-identical from the safety.viz v1.1.0 release bundle, pinned by version-suffixed directory (the gsm.kri `gsm.viz-2.3.0` precedent).
- Shared R helpers for the bindings: settings/payload construction, required-column validation against the module JSON data contracts, and a common `createWidget()` wrapper so the six bindings stay thin and consistent.
- Retire `RenderSafetyChartsWidget()` and drop `safetyCharts` + `Tendril` from Imports/Remotes; DESCRIPTION refreshed (version 1.0.0, jwildfire URLs, trimmed dependencies).

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — qcthat-linked testthat coverage (test names reference this issue per the v1.0.0 qualification matrix)
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — rendered example page published as RC evidence on the hub
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

Title: Add Widget_Histogram htmlwidget consuming the safety.viz bundle
Body:
Parent: jwildfire/obot.roadmap#2 (slice ③) · [Design document](https://jwildfire.github.io/obot.roadmap/requirements/design/2_design.html) · Depends on the safety.viz histogram module (slice ②) shipping a `dist/` bundle

## Summary

Add `Widget_Histogram`, the first safetyGraphics-replacement htmlwidget in gsm.safety: an interactive lab-results distribution view (normal-range overlays + linked participant listing) rendered by the safety.viz histogram module. Mirrors how gsm.kri wraps gsm.viz charts.

## Proposed Solution

- `inst/htmlwidgets/Widget_Histogram.js` + `Widget_Histogram.yaml`, with the safety.viz bundle vendored at `inst/htmlwidgets/lib/safety.viz-{version}/` (the gsm.kri precedent — no node toolchain at install time).
- `Widget_Histogram()` R function maps a long lab-results data.frame (`subjid`-keyed, per gsm column conventions) plus a settings list onto the module's JSON data contract, then drives the module lifecycle API (`init`/`setData`/`setSettings`/`render`/`resize`/`destroy`) from the widget's `renderValue`/`resize` hooks.

## Example Usage

```r
Widget_Histogram(
  dfResults = dfLabs,           # long format: one record per measurement
  lSettings = list(
    strMeasureCol = "lbtest",
    strResultCol  = "lbstresn",
    strIDCol      = "subjid"
  )
)
```

## QC Approach

In addition to standard QC (e.g., code reviews, automated checks) the following QC measures will be implemented:

- [ ] Unit Tests — testthat coverage of the R-side data/settings → JSON contract mapping
- [ ] User Tests (e.g. visual comparison) — pkgdown example page provides the rendered artifact for review
- [ ] `devtools::check()` clean as the merge gate

---

This Issue was drafted by Claude Code using Fable 5 and reviewed by @jwildfire.
Test: Widget_Histogram returns an htmlwidget carrying the histogram payload (#30)

Title: Add pkgdown widget thumbnail gallery
Body:
Add a pkgdown gallery for the interactive SafetyCharts examples.

Scope:
- Generate thumbnail screenshots for each supported interactive widget example.
- Replace or supplement the current widget table with a gallery of thumbnail cards.
- Each thumbnail should link to the corresponding pkgdown example.
- Prefer a headless-browser capture workflow so JavaScript/htmlwidgets finish rendering before capture.
- Keep screenshot tooling as development/docs tooling, not a runtime package dependency.

Initial implementation plan:
- Use Playwright to open rendered pkgdown example pages.
- Wait for htmlwidget content to render.
- Capture stable element/page screenshots into package assets.
- Render a gallery in README/pkgdown index with clickable thumbnail links.

Acceptance criteria:
- Gallery appears on the pkgdown home page.
- Gallery links go to the corresponding examples.
- Thumbnails are committed static assets.
- Documentation explains how to regenerate thumbnails.
Test: every exported widget has a gallery thumbnail (#28)
